summary: DeVault argues that tech workers must unionize immediately, framing it as both an economic necessity and a literal survival imperative. He contends that tech employers are systematically undermining worker power through mass layoffs, AI replacement, and flooding the labor market with cheaper graduates. Beyond workplace economics, he connects unionization to existential threats including climate change, healthcare failures, and rising fascism, arguing that organized labor is the only mechanism through which workers can exert meaningful power against capital. He provides a concrete step-by-step organizing guide and calls for tech workers to join the planned May 1st, 2028 general strike.
key_points: Tech employers are waging a deliberate war on labor through mass layoffs, AI mandates, coding bootcamps, and market flooding to make workers replaceable and cheap

Climate change, healthcare profiteering, and rising fascism represent existential threats that individual action and voting cannot address, as policy adoption is driven entirely by wealth, not public opinion
Organized labor has a proven track record of winning concessions — from UAW's 25% raises to the Writer's Guild's AI protections — and is the only strategy that consistently works against capital
Tech workers should build workplace solidarity, understand their legal rights, form unions with help from CODE-CWA and AFL-CIO, and prepare for the May 2028 general strike
The plutocracy's answer to climate migration and inequality will be fascism and concentration camps unless workers organize to force a different outcome
money_quotes: Tech workers, it's time for you to unionize, and strike, or you and your loved ones are literally going to die.
Don't let yourself believe you're a part of their club – if your income depends on your salary, you are part of the working class.
Your enemy isn't in the cubicle next to you, or on the other side of the open office. Your enemy has an office with a door on it.
The plutocracy has an answer to climate change: fascism.
Capitalism is going to murder the people you love. Capitalism is going to murder you.
To control the rich, you must threaten the only thing they care about.
They are more afraid of you than we are afraid of them. They are terrified of us, and it's time we used that to our advantage.
key_insight: Tech workers have unique leverage over the most powerful institutions in the world, and organized labor is the only proven mechanism to wield that power against existential threats from climate change to fascism.
